sexual_orientation.definition_in_act: Sexual orientation - means heterosexuality, homosexuality, bisexuality or asexuality

gender_identity.definition_in_act: Gender identity - in relation to a person, means the gender with which the person identifies
gender_identity.notes: Tasmania has one of the most inclusive gender identity definitions in Australia

intersex_status_or_sex_characteristics.definition_in_act: Intersex variations of sex characteristics - means a person's status as having physical, hormonal or genetic features that are: (a) neither wholly female nor wholly male; or (b) a combination of female and male; or (c) neither female nor male
intersex_status_or_sex_characteristics.notes: Tasmania was the first Australian jurisdiction to update terminology from 'intersex status' to 'intersex variations of sex characteristics'

areas_where_discrimination_is_unlawful: Provision of goods, services and facilities

religious_organisations.summary: Tasmania has the STRONGEST protections against religious discrimination in Australia. Religious exemptions only allow religious organisations to discriminate on the basis of people's religious beliefs, NOT on the basis of sexual orientation, gender identity or intersex variations of sex characteristics. This means LGBTIQ+ people are fully protected against discrimination by religious organisations, including religious schools.

small_employers.summary: No small employer exemption in Tasmania

complaint_body.name: Equal Opportunity Tasmania (formerly Anti-Discrimination Tasmania)
complaint_body.time_limit: 12 months from the act of discrimination (Commissioner may accept complaints after this period if reasonable)
complaint_body.costs_summary: No fee for lodging a complaint

statutory_basis: Anti-Discrimination Act 1998 (Tas), Part 3 - prohibited conduct including vilification
vilification.notes: Tasmania has comprehensive vilification protections for LGBTIQ+ people, covering sexual orientation (including asexual), gender identity (including non-binary), and intersex variations of sex characteristics. Tasmania is one of only 5 Australian jurisdictions with LGBTIQ+ vilification protections.

how_to_contact: Call 131 444 and ask for an LGBTI Liaison Officer
Visit your local police station and request an LGBTI Liaison Officer

key_points_summary: Sexual orientation (including asexual), gender identity (including non-binary) and intersex variations of sex characteristics are all protected
STRONGEST religious protections in Australia - religious organisations can ONLY discriminate on basis of religious belief, NOT on sexual orientation, gender identity or intersex status
LGBTIQ+ students and teachers in religious schools are fully protected
Comprehensive vilification protections for all LGBTIQ+ people
Tasmania is widely considered to have the best LGBTIQ+ anti-discrimination protections in Australia

sexual_orientation.what_this_means: You cannot be discriminated against because you are heterosexual, gay, lesbian, bisexual, or asexual.
real_world_examples: Your employer cannot fire you for being gay, lesbian, bisexual, or asexual
A landlord cannot refuse to rent to you because you're in a same-sex relationship
A hospital cannot deny you treatment based on sexual orientation
A school cannot expel your child for having same-sex parents
A religious school cannot refuse to hire you as a teacher just because you're gay
A café cannot refuse to serve you because you're in a same-sex relationship
sexual_orientation.limitations: None - Tasmania has the most comprehensive protections in Australia, explicitly including asexual people.

gender_identity.what_this_means: You cannot be discriminated against based on your gender identity, including if you are transgender, non-binary, genderfluid, agender, or any other gender identity.
real_world_examples: Your employer must use your correct name and pronouns
You cannot be denied employment because you are transitioning or non-binary
A landlord cannot refuse housing because you are trans or non-binary
Schools cannot prevent you from using facilities matching your gender identity
You cannot be refused service at a shop, restaurant, or doctor because of your gender identity
A religious school cannot refuse to hire you as a teacher just because you're trans or non-binary
A religious school cannot expel students for being trans or non-binary
gender_identity.limitations: None - Tasmania has one of the most inclusive gender identity definitions in Australia. Religious organisations can ONLY discriminate based on religious belief, NOT gender identity.

intersex_variations.what_this_means: You cannot be discriminated against based on intersex variations of sex characteristics (chromosomes, hormones, anatomy).
real_world_examples: You cannot be denied medical care based on being intersex
An employer cannot ask invasive questions about your body
You cannot be excluded from school activities because of sex characteristics
A gym cannot refuse you membership because you are intersex
A religious school cannot refuse to hire you just because you're intersex
intersex_variations.limitations: None - Tasmania was the first Australian jurisdiction to update terminology from 'intersex status' to 'intersex variations of sex characteristics' (2019).

what_youre_protected_from: Being rejected for a job because of sexual orientation/gender identity/intersex variations
Being paid less than colleagues because you're LGBTIQ+
Being harassed by coworkers or managers
Being forced to hide your identity at work
Being fired because you transition or come out
Religious organisations refusing to hire you just because you're LGBTIQ+ (they can only discriminate based on religious belief)
not_protected_from: Performance-based discipline (if you're underperforming for legitimate work reasons)

what_youre_protected_from: Schools refusing to enroll your child because you're a same-sex couple
Universities discriminating in admissions
Teachers intentionally using wrong pronouns
Being excluded from school events, sports, or facilities
Disciplinary action for being LGBTIQ+
Religious schools expelling students for being LGBTIQ+
Religious schools refusing to hire LGBTIQ+ teachers just because they're LGBTIQ+
Landlords refusing to rent because you're in a same-sex relationship
Being evicted because of your gender identity or intersex variations
Real estate agents refusing to show you properties
Being denied mortgage approval because you're LGBTIQ+
Being refused service at a café or restaurant
A doctor refusing to treat you
Being denied access to public facilities
Hotels refusing to book rooms for same-sex couples
Hairdressers refusing service
Religious organisations refusing to serve you just because you're LGBTIQ+
Being excluded from a sports team because of sexual orientation or gender identity
Being denied membership in a professional association
Being expelled from a club

religious_organisations.plain_english_summary: Tasmania has the STRONGEST protections against religious discrimination in Australia. Religious organisations can ONLY discriminate based on religious belief, NOT on sexual orientation, gender identity, or intersex variations.
what_they_can_do: Religious schools can require staff and students to uphold religious beliefs
Religious organisations can make decisions based on whether someone shares their religious faith
what_they_cannot_do: Religious schools cannot refuse to hire someone just for being gay, lesbian, bisexual, asexual, trans, non-binary, or intersex
Religious schools cannot expel students for being LGBTIQ+
Religious organisations cannot refuse services to LGBTIQ+ people
Religious hospitals cannot refuse to hire LGBTIQ+ staff just because they're LGBTIQ+
religious_organisations.real_example: A Catholic school can require teachers to attend Mass and uphold Catholic teachings, but cannot refuse to hire a teacher simply because they are gay, transgender, or intersex. The school can only make employment decisions based on religious belief, not LGBTIQ+ status. This is the strongest protection in Australia.
religious_organisations.if_you_face_discrimination: Tasmania has the best protections in Australia. If a religious organisation claims they can discriminate against you, they must prove it's based on religious belief, not LGBTIQ+ status. Seek legal advice from Equal Opportunity Tasmania.

jurisdiction_rating.rating_explanation: Tasmania has the strongest LGBTIQ+ protections in Australia. Sexual orientation (including asexual), gender identity (including non-binary), and intersex variations are all comprehensively protected. Religious organisations can ONLY discriminate based on religious belief, NOT LGBTIQ+ status. Comprehensive vilification protections exist. Tasmania is the gold standard for LGBTIQ+ rights in Australia.
strengths: Sexual orientation (including asexual), gender identity (including non-binary), and intersex variations all protected
STRONGEST religious protections in Australia - religious organisations can ONLY discriminate based on religious belief, NOT LGBTIQ+ status
LGBTIQ+ students and teachers in religious schools are fully protected
Comprehensive vilification protections for all LGBTIQ+ people
Widely considered the best LGBTIQ+ protections in Australia
gaps: None identified - Tasmania is the gold standard

secondary_analysis: Alastair Lawrie - A Quick Guide to Australian LGBTI Anti-Discrimination Laws
